layoutLoading.title: Opening the control center
layoutLoading.description: Kapter is verifying your Clerk session, restoring dashboard navigation, and syncing the last working surfaces before opening the control room.

pageHeader.title: Post-meeting operations dashboard
pageHeader.description: Review recordings, approve action items, and prepare the Notion sync.

coachmark.title: Need a simpler first-run view?
coachmark.description: You are still early in the workflow. Switch back to the beginner dashboard for a calmer, step-by-step surface.

guided.title: Get to your first reviewed meeting
guided.description: Kapter keeps this page focused on the next useful action so first-time users do not have to learn the whole operations dashboard at once.
guided.standardHint: Move to the standard dashboard anytime for full filters, project setup, and Notion destination management.

extension.title: Install the extension
extension.description: Use the extension to create the first meeting record and send it back here.

capture.title: Capture the meeting
capture.description: Join Google Meet, start recording, and let Kapter process the session.

review.title: Review the AI output
review.description: Open the meeting detail page, confirm the summary, and approve the action items.

handoff.title: Move into operations
handoff.description: Use the standard dashboard for deeper project management, queue work, and Notion setup.

primary.title: Your next step
primary.subtitle: One action at a time. Finish this step and Kapter will move you forward.
primary.installTitle: Install the extension before your first capture
primary.installDescription: The extension creates the first meeting record and brings it back here for review.
primary.installAdvanceHint: Kapter cannot inspect Chrome's extension list from the dashboard alone. If auto-detection does not happen right after you install the extension, refresh this dashboard tab or use “I have installed it” to move to the capture step.
primary.reviewDescription: {{title}} is ready for human review. Open it now to check the summary and action items.
primary.captureSetupTitle: Open Google Meet and start your first capture
primary.captureSetupDescription: Now that the extension is installed, open Google Meet, pin Kapter in Chrome, and start recording. The first meeting will appear here automatically once capture begins.
primary.captureSetupBackHint: If you still need to re-download the extension or reopen the setup instructions, go back to the install step.
primary.extensionDetectedTitle: Extension detected on this dashboard tab
primary.extensionDetectedHint: Kapter received a live response from the extension, so you can go straight to Google Meet and start the first capture.
primary.captureDescription: {{title}} is still recording or processing. Open it to monitor progress and stay close to the workflow.
primary.latestTitle: Open the latest meeting and continue
primary.latestDescription: {{title}} is the best next surface to keep your first workflow moving forward.
primary.handoffTitle: You are ready for the full operations dashboard
primary.handoffDescription: You already have a reviewed meeting. Move into the standard dashboard for project management, Notion setup, and the full meeting queue.

guided.secondaryTitle: What stays in the standard dashboard
guided.secondaryDescription: Project setup, Notion destinations, and deeper filters stay in the operations view so this beginner surface can stay focused.

recent.title: Recent meetings
recent.description: Keep the last few sessions nearby without opening the full filtered workspace.
recent.empty: Once the extension creates the first meeting, the latest sessions will appear here.

errorBanner.message: Connection error, data could not be loaded

notionCallbackBanner.connectedDescription: You can now assign a Notion destination to each project from the dashboard.
notionCallbackBanner.fallbackDescription: The Notion authentication flow did not complete.

meetingPanel.title: Meetings
meetingPanel.deleteDialogDescription: This permanently removes the meeting record and everything extracted from it.
meetingPanel.deleteMeetingHint: This will permanently delete the meeting, transcript fragments, extracted tasks, and project-memory proposals tied to {{project}}. This action cannot be undone.
meetingPanel.deleteMeetingError: Unable to delete the requested meeting.
meetingPanel.refreshError: Unable to refresh the meeting list.
meetingPanel.emptyWithFilters: Try clearing the filters to view the full history.
meetingPanel.emptyWithoutFilters: The extension will create the first record here.

projectPanel.title: Projects & Notion
projectPanel.description: Choose a project to narrow the meeting list.

projectsPanel.title: Projects
projectsPanel.description: Create work context so recordings and future tasks land in the right place.
projectsPanel.connectedDescription: Connected to {{workspace}}. Pick one shared page per project so Kapter can create the task database there.
projectsPanel.oauthDescription: Connect Notion once, then assign one page per project or let Kapter create a dedicated page.
projectsPanel.unavailableDescription: Notion OAuth is not configured in this environment. Projects stay local until Notion OAuth is set up.
projectsPanel.noProjectSelectedDescription: Select a project from the right rail to configure its Notion destination here.
projectsPanel.newProjectDescription: Start simple. You can add more context and connect Notion later.
projectsPanel.projectDescriptionPlaceholder: What is this project about? This helps the AI analyze it more accurately.
projectsPanel.deleteDialogDescription: Review everything that will be removed before continuing to the final confirmation.
projectsPanel.deleteProjectConfirmHint: This permanently removes the project brief, saved context, and destination metadata for this empty project.
projectsPanel.deleteProjectCascadeHint: This project still has {{count}} linked meeting(s). If you continue, Kapter will delete the project and every linked meeting, transcript fragment, extracted task, and project-memory proposal with it.
projectsPanel.deleteFinalDialogDescription: This destructive action cannot be undone after you confirm it.
projectsPanel.deleteProjectCascadeWarning: You are about to permanently delete this project and {{count}} linked meeting(s). All transcript data, extracted tasks, and memory proposals under those meetings will be removed as well.
projectsPanel.deleteProjectFinalHint: You are about to permanently delete this project and its saved context. This action cannot be undone.
projectsPanel.editDialogDescription: Keep the title, description, and memory seed aligned with the context future meetings should inherit.
projectsPanel.loadProjectDetailError: Unable to load the project details.
projectsPanel.initialDescriptionPlaceholder: The core goal, scope, or starting brief for this project.
projectsPanel.contextMarkdownPlaceholder: Background notes, durable decisions, domain language, or constraints Kapter should remember.
projectsPanel.emptyProjects: No projects yet. The extension can still record into the draft project, but creating a project here gives you stable context to reuse.
projectsPanel.missingProjectDescription: This project does not have a description yet. Meetings can still be attached to it.
projectsPanel.connectWorkspaceFirst: Connect the Notion workspace first, then return here to pick the parent page for this project.
projectsPanel.destinationModeDescription: Choose whether Kapter works inside the page you pick or creates a dedicated project page.
projectsPanel.createDedicatedPageDescription: Kapter creates a child project page and places the task database inside it.
projectsPanel.useExistingPageDescription: Kapter attaches the project to the page you pick and creates the task database there.
projectsPanel.searchSharedPagePlaceholder: Search for a page you already shared with Kapter
projectsPanel.searchPagesError: Unable to search shared Notion pages.
projectsPanel.selectPageBeforeSaving: Choose a shared Notion page before saving.
projectsPanel.saveDestinationError: Unable to save the Notion destination.
projectsPanel.clearDestinationError: Unable to clear the Notion destination.
projectsPanel.savedPageFallback: A page was already saved for this project.
